subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties

remember: Students will be able to recall the periodic table and its significance in the classification of elements.
Students will be able to identify the position of metal, non-metal and metalloid in the periodic table.
Students will be able to recall the modern periodic law and its significance.
understand: Students will be able to understand the relationship between atomic radius and periodic trends.
Students will be able to understand the factors affecting atomic radius.
Students will be able to explain the periodic trends in electronegativity and electron affinity.
apply: Students will be able to apply the periodic trends to predict the properties of elements.
Students will be able to use the periodic table to identify the elements with similar properties.
Students will be able to predict the reactivity series of elements.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the periodic trends and explain the variations in properties within a group and period.
Students will be able to explain the anomalies in periodic trends and their significance.
Students will be able to evaluate the periodic trends and their relationship with the electronic configuration of elements.

ncert_objectives: To understand the periodic table as an instrument for classification of elements.
To understand the relationship between the electronic configuration of atoms and their position in the periodic table.
To understand the periodic trends in atomic radius, electronegativity, and electron affinity.
board_exam_objectives: To identify the position of metals, non-metals, and metalloids in the periodic table.
To explain the periodic trends in atomic radius, electronegativity, and electron affinity.
To predict the properties and reactivity of elements using the periodic table.
